iso27001.title: Policies for information security
iso27001.description: Information security policy and topic-specific policies shall be defined, approved by management, published, communicated to and acknowledged by relevant personnel and relevant interested parties, and reviewed at planned intervals.

iso27001.title: Information security roles and responsibilities
iso27001.description: Information security roles and responsibilities shall be defined and allocated according to the organisation needs.

iso27001.title: Segregation of duties
iso27001.description: Conflicting duties and conflicting areas of responsibility shall be segregated.

iso27001.title: Management responsibilities
iso27001.description: Management shall require all personnel to apply information security in accordance with the established information security policy, topic-specific policies and procedures of the organisation.

iso27001.title: Contact with authorities
iso27001.description: The organisation shall establish and maintain contact with relevant authorities.

iso27001.title: Contact with special interest groups
iso27001.description: The organisation shall establish and maintain contact with special interest groups or other specialist security forums and professional associations.

iso27001.title: Threat intelligence
iso27001.description: Information relating to information security threats shall be collected and analysed to produce threat intelligence.

iso27001.title: Information security in project management
iso27001.description: Information security shall be integrated into project management.

iso27001.title: Inventory of information and other associated assets
iso27001.description: An inventory of information and other associated assets, including owners, shall be developed and maintained.

iso27001.title: Acceptable use of information and other associated assets
iso27001.description: Rules for the acceptable use and procedures for handling information and other associated assets shall be identified, documented and implemented.

iso27001.title: Return of assets
iso27001.description: Personnel and other interested parties shall return all the organisation's assets in their possession upon change or termination of their employment, contract or agreement.

iso27001.title: Classification of information
iso27001.description: Information shall be classified according to the information security needs of the organisation based on confidentiality, integrity, availability and relevant interested party requirements.

iso27001.title: Labelling of information
iso27001.description: An appropriate set of procedures for information labelling shall be developed and implemented in accordance with the information classification scheme adopted by the organisation.

iso27001.title: Information transfer
iso27001.description: Information transfer rules, procedures, or agreements shall be in place for all types of transfer facilities within the organisation and between the organisation and other parties.

iso27001.title: Access control
iso27001.description: Rules to control physical and logical access to information and other associated assets shall be established and implemented based on business and information security requirements.

iso27001.title: Identity management
iso27001.description: The full life cycle of identities shall be managed.

iso27001.title: Authentication information
iso27001.description: Allocation and management of authentication information shall be controlled by a management process, including advising personnel on appropriate handling of authentication information.

iso27001.title: Access rights
iso27001.description: Access rights to information and other associated assets shall be provisioned, reviewed, modified and removed in accordance with the organisation's topic-specific policy on and rules for access control.

iso27001.title: Information security in supplier relationships
iso27001.description: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the use of supplier's products or services.

iso27001.title: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
iso27001.description: Relevant information security requirements shall be established and agreed with each supplier based on the type of supplier relationship.

iso27001.title: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
iso27001.description: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the ICT products and services supply chain.

iso27001.title: Monitoring, review and change management of supplier services
iso27001.description: The organisation shall regularly monitor, review, evaluate and manage change in supplier information security practices and service delivery.

iso27001.title: Information security for use of cloud services
iso27001.description: Processes for acquisition, use, management and exit from cloud services shall be established in accordance with the organisation's information security requirements.

iso27001.title: Information security incident management planning and preparation
iso27001.description: The organisation shall plan and prepare for managing information security incidents by defining, establishing and communicating information security incident management processes, roles and responsibilities.

iso27001.title: Assessment and decision on information security events
iso27001.description: The organisation shall assess information security events and decide if they are to be categorised as information security incidents.

iso27001.title: Response to information security incidents
iso27001.description: Information security incidents shall be responded to in accordance with the documented procedures.

iso27001.title: Learning from information security incidents
iso27001.description: Knowledge gained from information security incidents shall be used to strengthen and improve the information security controls.

iso27001.title: Collection of evidence
iso27001.description: The organisation shall establish and implement procedures for the identification, collection, acquisition and preservation of evidence related to information security events.

iso27001.title: Information security during disruption
iso27001.description: The organisation shall plan how to maintain information security at an appropriate level during disruption.

iso27001.title: ICT readiness for business continuity
iso27001.description: ICT readiness shall be planned, implemented, maintained and tested based on business continuity objectives and ICT continuity requirements.

iso27001.title: Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements
iso27001.description: Legal, statutory, regulatory and contractual requirements relevant to information security and the organisation's approach to meet these requirements shall be identified, documented and kept up to date.

iso27001.title: Intellectual property rights
iso27001.description: The organisation shall implement appropriate procedures to protect intellectual property rights.

iso27001.title: Protection of records
iso27001.description: Records shall be protected from loss, destruction, falsification, unauthorised access and unauthorised release.

iso27001.title: Privacy and protection of personal identifiable information
iso27001.description: The organisation shall identify and meet the requirements regarding the preservation of privacy and protection of personal identifiable information according to applicable laws and regulations.

iso27001.title: Independent review of information security
iso27001.description: The organisation's approach to managing information security and its implementation including people, processes and technologies shall be reviewed independently at planned intervals or when significant changes occur.

iso27001.title: Compliance with policies, rules and standards for information security
iso27001.description: Compliance with the organisation's information security policy, topic-specific policies, rules and standards shall be regularly reviewed.

iso27001.title: Documented operating procedures
iso27001.description: Operating procedures for information processing facilities shall be documented and made available to personnel who need them.

iso27001.title: Screening
iso27001.description: Background verification checks on all candidates to become personnel shall be carried out prior to joining the organisation and on an ongoing basis taking into consideration applicable laws, regulations and ethics.

iso27001.title: Terms and conditions of employment
iso27001.description: The employment contractual agreements shall state the personnel's and the organisation's responsibilities for information security.

iso27001.title: Information security awareness, education and training
iso27001.description: Personnel of the organisation and relevant interested parties shall receive appropriate information security awareness, education and training and regular updates of the organisation's information security policy, topic-specific policies and procedures, as relevant for their job function.

iso27001.title: Disciplinary process
iso27001.description: A disciplinary process shall be formalised and communicated to take actions against personnel and other relevant interested parties who have committed an information security policy violation.

iso27001.title: Responsibilities after termination or change of employment
iso27001.description: Information security responsibilities and duties that remain valid after termination or change of employment shall be defined, enforced and communicated to relevant personnel and other interested parties.

iso27001.title: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements
iso27001.description: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements reflecting the organisation's needs for the protection of information shall be identified, documented, regularly reviewed and signed by personnel and other relevant interested parties.

iso27001.title: Remote working
iso27001.description: Security measures shall be implemented when personnel are working remotely to protect information accessed, processed or stored outside the organisation's premises.

iso27001.title: Information security event reporting
iso27001.description: The organisation shall provide a mechanism for personnel to report observed or suspected information security events through appropriate channels in a timely manner.

iso27001.title: Physical security perimeters
iso27001.description: Security perimeters shall be defined and used to protect areas that contain information and other associated assets.

iso27001.title: Physical entry
iso27001.description: Secure areas shall be protected by appropriate entry controls and access points.

iso27001.title: Securing offices, rooms and facilities
iso27001.description: Physical security for offices, rooms and facilities shall be designed and implemented.

iso27001.title: Physical security monitoring
iso27001.description: Premises shall be continuously monitored for unauthorised physical access.

iso27001.title: Protecting against physical and environmental threats
iso27001.description: Protection against physical and environmental threats, such as natural disasters and other intentional or unintentional physical threats to infrastructure shall be designed and implemented.

iso27001.title: Working in secure areas
iso27001.description: Security measures for working in secure areas shall be designed and implemented.

iso27001.title: Clear desk and clear screen
iso27001.description: Clear desk rules for papers and removable storage media and clear screen rules for information processing facilities shall be defined and appropriately enforced.

iso27001.title: Equipment siting and protection
iso27001.description: Equipment shall be sited securely and protected.

iso27001.title: Security of assets off-premises
iso27001.description: Off-site assets shall be protected.

iso27001.title: Storage media
iso27001.description: Storage media shall be managed through their life cycle of acquisition, use, transportation and disposal in accordance with the organisation's classification scheme and handling requirements.

iso27001.title: Supporting utilities
iso27001.description: Information processing facilities shall be protected from power failures and other disruptions caused by failures in supporting utilities.

iso27001.title: Cabling security
iso27001.description: Cables carrying power, data or supporting information services shall be protected from interception, interference or damage.

iso27001.title: Equipment maintenance
iso27001.description: Equipment shall be maintained correctly to ensure availability, integrity and confidentiality of information.

iso27001.title: Secure disposal or re-use of equipment
iso27001.description: Items of equipment containing storage media shall be verified to ensure that any sensitive data and licensed software has been removed or securely overwritten prior to disposal or re-use.

iso27001.title: User endpoint devices
iso27001.description: Information stored on, processed by or accessible via user endpoint devices shall be protected.

iso27001.title: Privileged access rights
iso27001.description: The allocation and use of privileged access rights shall be restricted and managed.

iso27001.title: Information access restriction
iso27001.description: Access to information and other associated assets shall be restricted in accordance with the established topic-specific policy on access control.

iso27001.title: Access to source code
iso27001.description: Read and write access to source code, development tools and software libraries shall be appropriately managed.

iso27001.title: Secure authentication
iso27001.description: Secure authentication technologies and procedures shall be implemented based on information access restrictions and the topic-specific policy on access control.

iso27001.title: Capacity management
iso27001.description: The use of resources shall be monitored and adjusted in line with current and expected capacity requirements.

iso27001.title: Protection against malware
iso27001.description: Protection against malware shall be implemented and supported by appropriate user awareness.

iso27001.title: Management of technical vulnerabilities
iso27001.description: Information about technical vulnerabilities of information systems in use shall be obtained in a timely fashion, the organisation's exposure to such vulnerabilities evaluated and appropriate measures taken.

iso27001.title: Configuration management
iso27001.description: Configurations, including security configurations, of hardware, software, services and networks shall be established, documented, implemented, monitored and reviewed.

iso27001.title: Information deletion
iso27001.description: Information stored in information systems, devices or in any other storage media shall be deleted when no longer required.

iso27001.title: Data masking
iso27001.description: Data masking shall be used in accordance with the organisation's topic-specific policy on access control and other related topic-specific policies, and business requirements, taking applicable legislation into consideration.

iso27001.title: Data leakage prevention
iso27001.description: Data leakage prevention measures shall be applied to systems, networks and any other devices that process, store or transmit sensitive information.

iso27001.title: Information backup
iso27001.description: Backup copies of information, software and systems shall be maintained and regularly tested in accordance with the agreed topic-specific policy on backup.

iso27001.title: Redundancy of information processing facilities
iso27001.description: Information processing facilities shall be implemented with redundancy sufficient to meet availability requirements.

iso27001.title: Logging
iso27001.description: Logs that record activities, exceptions, faults and other relevant events shall be produced, stored, protected and analysed.

iso27001.title: Monitoring activities
iso27001.description: Networks, systems and applications shall be monitored for anomalous behaviour and appropriate actions taken to evaluate potential information security incidents.

iso27001.title: Clock synchronisation
iso27001.description: The clocks of information processing systems used by the organisation shall be synchronised to approved time sources.

iso27001.title: Use of privileged utility programs
iso27001.description: The use of utility programs that can be capable of overriding system and application controls shall be restricted and tightly controlled.

iso27001.title: Installation of software on operational systems
iso27001.description: Procedures and measures shall be implemented to securely manage software installation on operational systems.

iso27001.title: Networks security
iso27001.description: Networks and network devices shall be secured, managed and controlled to protect information in systems and applications.

iso27001.title: Security of network services
iso27001.description: Security mechanisms, service levels and service requirements of network services shall be identified, implemented and monitored.

iso27001.title: Segregation of networks
iso27001.description: Groups of information services, users and information systems shall be segregated in the organisation's networks.

iso27001.title: Web filtering
iso27001.description: Access to external websites shall be managed to reduce exposure to malicious content.

iso27001.title: Use of cryptography
iso27001.description: Rules for the effective use of cryptography, including cryptographic key management, shall be defined and implemented.

iso27001.title: Secure development life cycle
iso27001.description: Rules for the secure development of software and systems shall be established and applied.

iso27001.title: Application security requirements
iso27001.description: Information security requirements shall be identified, specified and approved when developing or acquiring applications.

iso27001.title: Secure system architecture and engineering principles
iso27001.description: Principles for engineering secure systems shall be established, documented, maintained and applied to any information system development or integration activities.

iso27001.title: Secure coding
iso27001.description: Secure coding principles shall be applied to software development.

iso27001.title: Security testing in development and acceptance
iso27001.description: Security testing processes shall be defined and implemented in the development life cycle.

iso27001.title: Outsourced development
iso27001.description: The organisation shall direct, monitor and review the activities related to outsourced system development.

iso27001.title: Separation of development, test and production environments
iso27001.description: Development, testing and production environments shall be separated and secured.

iso27001.title: Change management
iso27001.description: Changes to information processing facilities and information systems shall be subject to change management procedures.

iso27001.title: Test information
iso27001.description: Test information shall be appropriately selected, protected and managed.

iso27001.title: Protection of information systems during audit testing
iso27001.description: Audit tests and other assurance activities involving assessment of operational systems shall be planned and agreed between the tester and appropriate management.

nis2.title: Policies on risk analysis and information system security
nis2.description: Essential and important entities shall implement cybersecurity risk-management measures encompassing policies on risk analysis and information system security.

nis2.title: Incident handling
nis2.description: Measures for incident handling including incident prevention, detection, and response, as well as the recovery from incidents.

nis2.title: Business continuity and crisis management
nis2.description: Business continuity, such as backup management and disaster recovery, and crisis management.

nis2.title: Supply chain security
nis2.description: Security in network and information systems acquisition, development and maintenance including vulnerability handling and disclosure.

nis2.title: Security in network and information systems acquisition
nis2.description: Security in network and information systems acquisition, development and maintenance including vulnerability handling and disclosure.

nis2.title: Policies and procedures to assess effectiveness of cybersecurity measures
nis2.description: Policies and procedures to assess the effectiveness of cybersecurity risk-management measures.

nis2.title: Basic cyber hygiene practices and cybersecurity training
nis2.description: Basic cyber hygiene practices and cybersecurity training.

nis2.title: Policies and procedures regarding cryptography
nis2.description: Policies and procedures regarding the use of cryptography and, where appropriate, encryption.

nis2.title: Human resources security, access control policies and asset management
nis2.description: Human resources security, access control policies and asset management.

nis2.title: Use of multi-factor authentication
nis2.description: Use of multi-factor authentication or continuous authentication solutions, secured voice, video and text communications and secured emergency communication systems.

nis2.title: Incident notification obligations
nis2.description: Essential and important entities shall notify the CSIRT or competent authority of any significant incident within specific timeframes.

nis2.title: Early warning and incident notification
nis2.description: Early warning within 24 hours, notification within 72 hours, and final report within one month of becoming aware of a significant incident.

nis2.title: Governance — management body responsibility
nis2.description: Member States shall ensure that the management bodies of essential and important entities approve the cybersecurity risk-management measures and oversee implementation.

nis2.title: Governance — management body training
nis2.description: Management body members shall follow training and encourage personnel to acquire sufficient cybersecurity knowledge and skills on a regular basis.

gdpr.title: Integrity and confidentiality
gdpr.description: Personal data shall be processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security of the personal data, including protection against unauthorised or unlawful processing and against accidental loss, destruction or damage, using appropriate technical or organisational measures.

gdpr.title: Data protection by design and by default
gdpr.description: The controller shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures for ensuring that, by default, only personal data which are necessary for each specific purpose of the processing are processed.

gdpr.title: Records of processing activities
gdpr.description: Each controller shall maintain a record of processing activities under its responsibility.

gdpr.title: Security of processing
gdpr.description: The controller and processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including pseudonymisation, encryption, ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability and resilience of processing systems.

gdpr.title: Notification of personal data breach to supervisory authority
gdpr.description: In the case of a personal data breach, the controller shall notify the competent supervisory authority within 72 hours of having become aware of it.

gdpr.title: Communication of personal data breach to data subject
gdpr.description: When a personal data breach is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall communicate the breach to the data subject without undue delay.

gdpr.title: Data protection impact assessment
gdpr.description: The controller shall carry out a data protection impact assessment where processing is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons.

gdpr.title: Designation of the data protection officer
gdpr.description: The controller and processor shall designate a data protection officer where the core activities consist of processing operations which require regular and systematic monitoring of data subjects.

gdpr.title: Processor
gdpr.description: Where processing is to be carried out on behalf of a controller, the controller shall use only processors providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures.

dora.title: ICT risk management framework
dora.description: Financial entities shall have in place a sound, comprehensive and well-documented ICT risk management framework as part of their overall risk management system.

dora.title: ICT risk management — systems and tools
dora.description: Financial entities shall use and maintain updated ICT systems, protocols and tools that are appropriate, reliable and sufficient to address the ICT risk.

dora.title: ICT asset management
dora.description: Financial entities shall identify, classify and document all ICT supported business functions and the ICT assets supporting them.

dora.title: ICT risk identification
dora.description: Financial entities shall identify and classify ICT-related incidents, continuously perform risk and vulnerability assessments with respect to all ICT systems.

dora.title: Protection and prevention
dora.description: Financial entities shall put in place appropriate ICT security policies, procedures, protocols and tools to protect ICT systems and ICT-enabled business processes.

dora.title: Detection
dora.description: Financial entities shall have in place mechanisms to promptly detect anomalous activities including ICT network performance issues and ICT-related incidents.

dora.title: Response and recovery
dora.description: Financial entities shall have in place a comprehensive ICT business continuity policy as part of their sound business continuity management system.

dora.title: Backup policies and restoration
dora.description: Financial entities shall have backup policies and procedures, backup and restoration methods, to ensure that data and ICT systems can be restored.

dora.title: Learning and evolving
dora.description: Financial entities shall gather information on vulnerabilities and cyber threats and conduct post-ICT-related incident reviews after significant disruptions.

dora.title: ICT-related incident management process
dora.description: Financial entities shall define, establish and implement an ICT-related incident management process to detect, manage and notify ICT-related incidents.

dora.title: Reporting of major ICT-related incidents
dora.description: Financial entities shall report major ICT-related incidents to the relevant competent authority within timeframes specified in the regulation.

dora.title: General requirements for digital operational resilience testing
dora.description: Financial entities shall establish, maintain and review a sound and comprehensive digital operational resilience testing programme.

dora.title: ICT third-party risk management
dora.description: Financial entities shall manage ICT third-party risk as an integral component of ICT risk within their ICT risk management framework.

dora.title: Key contractual provisions for ICT third-party service providers
dora.description: Rights and obligations of the financial entity and of the ICT third-party service provider shall be clearly allocated and set out in a written arrangement.

cis.title: Inventory and Control of Enterprise Assets
cis.description: Actively manage all enterprise assets connected to the infrastructure physically, virtually, remotely, and those within cloud environments to accurately know the totality of assets that need to be monitored and protected.

cis.title: Inventory and Control of Software Assets
cis.description: Actively manage all software on the network so that only authorised software is installed and can execute, and that unauthorised and unmanaged software is found and prevented from installation or execution.

cis.title: Data Protection
cis.description: Develop processes and technical controls to identify, classify, securely handle, retain, and dispose of data.

cis.title: Secure Configuration of Enterprise Assets and Software
cis.description: Establish and maintain the secure configuration of enterprise assets and software.

cis.title: Account Management
cis.description: Use processes and tools to assign and manage authorisation to credentials for user accounts, including administrator accounts, as well as service accounts, to enterprise assets and software.

cis.title: Access Control Management
cis.description: Use processes and tools to create, assign, manage, and revoke access credentials and privileges for user, administrator, and service accounts for enterprise assets and software.

cis.title: Continuous Vulnerability Management
cis.description: Develop a plan to continuously assess and track vulnerabilities on all enterprise assets within the infrastructure in order to remediate and minimise the window of opportunity for attackers.

cis.title: Audit Log Management
cis.description: Collect, alert, review, and retain audit logs of events that could help detect, understand, or recover from an attack.

cis.title: Email and Web Browser Protections
cis.description: Improve protections and detections of threats from email and web vectors, as these are opportunities for attackers to manipulate human behaviour.

cis.title: Malware Defences
cis.description: Prevent or control the installation, spread, and execution of malicious applications, code, or scripts on enterprise assets.

cis.title: Data Recovery
cis.description: Establish and maintain data recovery practices sufficient to restore in-scope enterprise assets to a pre-incident and trusted state.

cis.title: Network Infrastructure Management
cis.description: Establish, implement, and actively manage network devices, in order to prevent attackers from exploiting vulnerable network services and access points.

cis.title: Network Monitoring and Defence
cis.description: Operate processes and tooling to establish and maintain comprehensive network monitoring and defence against security threats.

cis.title: Security Awareness and Skills Training
cis.description: Establish and maintain a security awareness programme to influence behaviour among the workforce to be security conscious and properly skilled.

cis.title: Service Provider Management
cis.description: Develop a process to evaluate service providers who hold sensitive data or are responsible for an enterprise's critical IT platforms or processes.

cis.title: Application Software Security
cis.description: Manage the security life cycle of in-house developed, hosted, or acquired software to prevent, detect, and remediate security weaknesses.

cis.title: Incident Response Management
cis.description: Establish a programme to develop and maintain an incident response capability in order to prepare, detect, contain, and eradicate attacks.

cis.title: Penetration Testing
cis.description: Test the effectiveness and resiliency of enterprise assets through identifying and exploiting weaknesses in controls, and simulating the objectives and actions of an attacker.

nistcsf.title: Organisational Context
nistcsf.description: The circumstances surrounding the organisation's cybersecurity risk management decisions are understood.

nistcsf.title: Risk Management Strategy
nistcsf.description: The organisation's priorities, constraints, risk tolerance and appetite statements, and assumptions are established, communicated, and used to support operational risk decisions.

nistcsf.title: Roles, Responsibilities, and Authorities
nistcsf.description: Cybersecurity roles, responsibilities, and authorities to foster accountability, performance assessment, and continuous improvement are established and communicated.

nistcsf.title: Policy
nistcsf.description: Organisational cybersecurity policy is established, communicated, and enforced.

nistcsf.title: Oversight
nistcsf.description: Results of organisation-wide cybersecurity risk management activities and performance are used to inform, improve, and adjust the risk management strategy.

nistcsf.title: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
nistcsf.description: Cyber supply chain risk management processes are identified, established, managed, monitored, and improved by organisational stakeholders.

nistcsf.title: Asset Management
nistcsf.description: Assets that enable the organisation to achieve business purposes are identified and managed consistent with their relative importance to organisational objectives and the organisation's risk strategy.

nistcsf.title: Risk Assessment
nistcsf.description: The cybersecurity risk to the organisation, assets, and individuals is understood by the organisation.

nistcsf.title: Improvement
nistcsf.description: Improvements to organisational cybersecurity risk management processes, procedures and activities are identified across all CSF Functions.

nistcsf.title: Identity Management, Authentication and Access Control
nistcsf.description: Access to physical and logical assets is limited to authorised users, services, and hardware and managed commensurate with the assessed risk of unauthorised access.

nistcsf.title: Awareness and Training
nistcsf.description: The organisation's personnel are provided with cybersecurity awareness and training so that they can perform their cybersecurity-related tasks.

nistcsf.title: Data Security
nistcsf.description: Data are managed consistent with the organisation's risk strategy to protect the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information.

nistcsf.title: Platform Security
nistcsf.description: The hardware, software, and services of physical and virtual platforms are managed consistent with the organisation's risk strategy to protect their confidentiality, integrity, and availability.

nistcsf.title: Technology Infrastructure Resilience
nistcsf.description: Security architectures are managed with the organisation's risk strategy to protect asset confidentiality, integrity, and availability and organisational resilience.

nistcsf.title: Continuous Monitoring
nistcsf.description: Assets are monitored to find anomalies, indicators of compromise, and other potentially adverse events.

nistcsf.title: Adverse Event Analysis
nistcsf.description: Anomalies, indicators of compromise, and other potentially adverse events are analysed to characterise the events and detect cybersecurity incidents.

nistcsf.title: Incident Management
nistcsf.description: Responses to detected cybersecurity incidents are managed.

nistcsf.title: Incident Analysis
nistcsf.description: Investigations are conducted to ensure effective response and support forensics and recovery activities.

nistcsf.title: Incident Response Reporting and Communication
nistcsf.description: Response activities are coordinated with internal and external stakeholders as required by laws, regulations, or policies.

nistcsf.title: Incident Mitigation
nistcsf.description: Activities are performed to prevent expansion of an event and mitigate its effects.

nistcsf.title: Incident Recovery Plan Execution
nistcsf.description: Restoration activities are performed to ensure operational availability of systems and services affected by cybersecurity incidents.

nistcsf.title: Incident Recovery Communication
nistcsf.description: Restoration activities are coordinated with internal and external parties.
